name: Udhari App

features: Versioned contracts approved by both people
Flexible multi-phase interest and repayment terms
Payments, wallet ledger, AutoPay, and balance views
Typed repository architecture ready for a secure backend

name: Intelligent Report Builder
description: An AI-intelligence workspace that turns natural-language intent and structured data into polished resumes, biodata, medical records, and business reports—then helps teams build templates, validate quality, investigate failures, and predict delivery time.
features: Natural-language report requests with missing-input follow-ups
AI template drafting, enhancement, repair, and save guardrails
Reusable output for resumes, biodata, healthcare, and business reports
Report quality and compliance review with actionable findings
Audit root-cause analysis across the report lifecycle
Statistical SLA and ETA prediction without an LLM at serving time
aiCapabilities: Report analysis & quality review · analyze-report / report-quality-review

name: Sponsor App
description: App for managing sponsorships and partnerships.

name: Auditor App
description: Comprehensive financial auditing platform that transforms bank statement analysis through intelligent automation. Features include PDF parsing for transaction extraction, transaction merging with duplicate removal, multi-bank statement management, cross-bank transaction grouping, fraud detection algorithms, and interactive visualization dashboards for comprehensive financial analysis and decision-making.

name: Cards Game
description: A cross-platform real-time card table for web, Android, and iOS, built around authoritative multiplayer rules, private tables, reconnect-safe sessions, and culturally familiar games including Chowgdi and Simple Capture.
features: Authoritative Go server for deterministic multiplayer rules
Real-time table state over HTTP and WebSocket
Private tables, retained seats, and reconnect grace periods
Two-player Simple Capture and four-player Chowgdi
Shared Expo client for web, Android, and iOS
Redis-backed lobby, session, table, and game state

name: Exam Prep - Study Assistant
description: Focused preparation assistance with weekly progress tracking, interactive quizzes, study analytics, and personalized learning paths.

name: Resume Builder
description: Advanced resume builder with customizable templates, export options, and modern design layouts.

name: Bio Data Builder
description: Professional bio-data creation tool with customizable templates, export options, and modern design layouts.

name: Portfolio Website Builder
description: Drag-and-drop portfolio website builder with customizable themes, content management, and hosting integration.

name: AI Dashboard Builder
description: Build custom dashboards with AI assistance - just upload any file and get intelligent visualizations and insights.

name: Productivity App
description: A comprehensive productivity application with task management, time tracking, habit building, and performance analytics.

name: AI Assistant
description: An AI-driven assistant that keeps personal relationships, finances, and work in three focused modes, then brings in the right specialist perspective—from software architecture and accounting to cooking and creative work—without mixing contexts.
features: Personal Relations mode for thoughtful communication, important moments, shared plans, and relationship context
Finance mode for budgeting, expense understanding, financial planning, and decision support
Work mode for planning, problem-solving, documentation, prioritization, and professional decisions
Current specialist roles: Software Architect, Chartered Accountant, Chef, and Artist
Intent-aware routing that selects the right mode, specialist, tools, and knowledge source for each request
Separate memory and permissions for personal, financial, and work contexts
Human-in-the-loop confirmations before sensitive recommendations or external actions
Expandable specialist marketplace for adding new professional roles over time

assistantModes.name: Personal Relations
assistantModes.detail: Helps prepare thoughtful messages, remember meaningful moments, plan shared activities, and reflect on conversations without pretending to replace human judgement.

assistantModes.name: Finance
assistantModes.detail: Turns transactions, budgets, goals, and financial documents into understandable summaries, options, reminders, and questions to discuss with a qualified professional.

assistantModes.name: Work
assistantModes.detail: Supports planning, research, architecture decisions, documentation, meeting preparation, prioritisation, and follow-through while keeping company context separate.

specialistRoles.name: Software Architect
specialistRoles.detail: Design reviews, trade-off analysis, system decomposition, API and data modelling, reliability, security, and technical decision records.

specialistRoles.name: Chartered Accountant
specialistRoles.detail: Financial categorisation, cash-flow interpretation, document checklists, compliance-oriented questions, and explainable calculations with clear professional disclaimers.

specialistRoles.name: Chef
specialistRoles.detail: Meal ideas based on ingredients, dietary preferences, time and budget, with practical substitutions, preparation plans, and shopping lists.

specialistRoles.name: Artist
specialistRoles.detail: Creative briefs, concept exploration, style references, critique, iteration prompts, and project planning across visual and expressive work.

architecture.name: Conversation gateway
architecture.detail: A React or mobile client sends a request with an explicitly selected mode. FastAPI handles identity, sessions, streaming responses, rate limits, and request validation.

architecture.name: Mode and intent router
architecture.detail: A small classifier and LangGraph workflow determine the permitted context, specialist role, retrieval sources, tools, and response policy for the request.

architecture.name: Specialist agent registry
architecture.detail: Each specialist is a versioned configuration containing instructions, allowed tools, knowledge collections, output schemas, confidence rules, and safety boundaries.

architecture.name: Separated memory
architecture.detail: Personal, finance, and work memories use distinct namespaces, encryption keys, retention controls, and access policies so information cannot leak across modes.

architecture.name: RAG and tool layer
architecture.detail: Vector retrieval grounds answers in user-approved documents. Tool calling handles calculations, calendars, tasks, recipes, code analysis, and other deterministic actions.

architecture.name: Trust and evaluation
architecture.detail: Citations, uncertainty, confirmation gates, audit logs, prompt and retrieval tests, specialist scorecards, and user feedback make behaviour measurable and reviewable.

skillsRequired.name: AI product design
skillsRequired.detail: Define jobs-to-be-done, decide when AI should assist or abstain, design confirmation moments, and make uncertainty understandable.

skillsRequired.name: LLM orchestration
skillsRequired.detail: Prompt and context engineering, structured outputs, LangGraph state machines, tool calling, retries, fallbacks, and model routing.

skillsRequired.name: RAG and data
skillsRequired.detail: Document ingestion, chunking, embeddings, metadata filters, hybrid retrieval, reranking, citations, and evaluation datasets.

skillsRequired.name: Security and privacy
skillsRequired.detail: Tenant isolation, consent, encryption, secrets, fine-grained access, data deletion, auditability, and protection against prompt injection.

skillsRequired.name: Backend engineering
skillsRequired.detail: FastAPI services, asynchronous jobs, event processing, PostgreSQL, vector storage, caching, observability, and reliable integrations.

skillsRequired.name: AI evaluation
skillsRequired.detail: Golden test cases, groundedness and tool-use checks, hallucination tracking, safety tests, cost and latency measurement, and feedback analysis.

deliveryPhases: MVP: launch Work mode with the Software Architect specialist, explicit mode selection, short-lived sessions, structured answers, and no autonomous external actions.
Grounding: add user-approved document ingestion, source citations, separated memory, retrieval evaluation, feedback capture, and privacy controls.
Specialists: introduce Chef and Artist with narrow tools and test suites; add Finance only after calculation checks, disclaimers, audit logs, and expert-reviewed boundaries are ready.
Personalisation: add opt-in long-term preferences, user-editable memories, specialist hand-offs, proactive suggestions, and configurable notification rules.
Scale: route requests across models by complexity, cache safe retrieval results, move long tasks to queues, measure cost per successful outcome, and add specialists from a governed registry.
constraints: Personal, financial, and workplace information must never cross mode boundaries unless the user explicitly approves a specific hand-off.
Finance guidance must show assumptions, calculations, sources, uncertainty, and when a qualified professional is required.
The assistant should request confirmation before sending messages, changing calendars, moving money, publishing content, or performing any consequential action.
Long-term memory must be opt-in, visible, editable, exportable, and deletable—not an invisible transcript archive.
Specialist quality needs domain-specific evaluation; a fluent response is not evidence that the recommendation is correct.

name: University Peer Cloud
description: A campus-owned cloud platform where a college control plane securely schedules student projects across opt-in student computers, turning spare compute into a shared application-hosting pool without running workloads on the administration server.
features: Self-service project deployment for every student through a campus developer portal
Opt-in node agent that lends bounded CPU, memory, storage, and network capacity
College-hosted control plane for scheduling, health checks, quotas, service discovery, and policy
Container isolation so hosted applications cannot access the student’s files or local network
Heartbeat-based failover and replica placement across independently available student computers
Campus ingress, TLS, and application URLs routed only to healthy student-hosted replicas
Usage credits and contribution points based on verified uptime and requests served
Stateless-first hosting with replicated storage introduced only for workloads that need persistence

architecture.name: College control plane
architecture.detail: API gateway, identity, deployment registry, scheduler, quota manager, health monitor, policy engine, and audit log. It orchestrates workloads but does not execute student applications.

architecture.name: Student node agent
architecture.detail: An opt-in background service advertises available resources, enforces lending limits, sends signed heartbeats, pulls approved images, and manages the local container sandbox.

architecture.name: Secure campus network
architecture.detail: WireGuard-based overlay networking gives every node and workload a private identity without exposing student devices directly to the internet.

architecture.name: Workload runtime
architecture.detail: Containerd or Docker runs resource-limited containers; k3s provides scheduling primitives, replica placement, service discovery, and controlled restarts.

architecture.name: Ingress and routing
architecture.detail: Traefik terminates TLS at the college edge and forwards requests only to healthy replicas discovered through the control plane.

architecture.name: Observability and accounting
architecture.detail: OpenTelemetry, Prometheus, and Grafana track health, latency, resource consumption, uptime, and contribution credits.

skillsRequired.name: Distributed systems
skillsRequired.detail: Heartbeats, failure detection, leader election, eventual consistency, replication, idempotency, and capacity-aware scheduling.

skillsRequired.name: Containers and Linux
skillsRequired.detail: Namespaces, cgroups, image lifecycle, process isolation, resource limits, and secure container execution.

skillsRequired.name: Networking
skillsRequired.detail: Overlay networks, DNS, service discovery, reverse proxies, TLS, NAT traversal, and load balancing.

skillsRequired.name: Cloud security
skillsRequired.detail: Zero-trust node identity, signed workloads, secrets management, least privilege, policy enforcement, and tenant isolation.

skillsRequired.name: Platform engineering
skillsRequired.detail: Developer portals, deployment APIs, workload manifests, quotas, rollouts, logs, and self-service workflows.

skillsRequired.name: SRE and operations
skillsRequired.detail: SLOs, monitoring, alerting, incident response, graceful degradation, capacity planning, and abuse controls.

deliveryPhases: MVP: host stateless HTTP applications on lab and student computers with explicit opt-in and fixed resource limits.
Reliability: add multiple replicas, health-based rescheduling, rolling deployments, logs, metrics, and campus-wide ingress.
Trust: add signed images, SPIFFE identities, OPA policies, vulnerability scanning, secret delivery, and per-project network rules.
Stateful workloads: introduce replicated volumes only after the stateless platform is reliable and operationally understood.
constraints: Student computers are intermittent, heterogeneous, and frequently behind restrictive networks.
The platform must never use resources beyond the owner’s explicit limits or interfere with normal device use.
Untrusted student code requires strong isolation, image scanning, egress controls, and rapid revocation.
Reliable state is significantly harder than stateless compute and should not be part of the first release.
College policy, consent, acceptable-use rules, privacy, and academic-integrity controls are product requirements—not paperwork after launch.

name: Machine Learning Projects
description: A collection of University Projects built while learning applied machine learning, covering 20+ real-world datasets across classification, regression, computer vision, exploratory analysis, feature engineering, and model evaluation.
features: Titanic survival prediction using passenger and demographic features
Black Friday purchase analysis and customer-behaviour modelling
House-price prediction using regression and structured property data
Cat-versus-dog image classification using computer vision models
Handwritten digit recognition using image classification
Driver drowsiness detection using facial and eye-state signals
Applied data cleaning, exploratory analysis, feature engineering, training, validation, and model evaluation across 20+ datasets

name: Jobs@LPU
description: A university hackathon project developed during the second year of college to help students discover paid gig opportunities and help people hire trusted student talent for short-term work.
features: Developed during a university hackathon in the second year of college
Student profiles highlighting skills, interests, availability, and previous work
Gig listings for short-term campus and community opportunities
Search and discovery by skill, category, availability, and budget
Simple applications connecting interested students with people hiring
Opportunity tracking for posted, applied, active, and completed gigs
Campus-focused trust model for hiring student gig workers

name: LLM-Powered Semantic Query Agent
description: An AI query agent that converts plain English into safe SQL using pgvector-based semantic schema retrieval, reducing prompt-token cost by roughly 90% compared with injecting an entire database schema.
features: Converts plain-English questions into database-ready SQL
Retrieves only relevant tables and columns through semantic schema search
Uses rule-based intent checks before invoking the generation pipeline
Builds pgvector embeddings for schema-aware retrieval
Generates SQL with an LLM using bounded, relevant context
Runs EXPLAIN dry-run validation before executing generated queries
Cuts prompt-token consumption by approximately 90% over full-schema injection

name: Agentic AI Support Automation Platform
description: A backend-focused Agentic AI prototype that combines LangGraph orchestration, RAG, tool calling, MCP, and vector search to reason over support documents and invoke governed backend APIs.
features: Orchestrates multi-step support reasoning with LangGraph
Retrieves grounded context from support documents and knowledge sources
Calls backend tools and APIs through explicit, governed contracts
Uses MCP-compatible integrations to extend available capabilities
Combines vector retrieval with conversational context
Separates reasoning, retrieval, tool execution, and response generation
Designed for backend-first automation, observability, and safe failure handling

name: E-commerce Product QA using Product Association Graph
description: A product question-answering system built over more than 100,000 e-commerce records, using association-graph retrieval, RAG, and context-aware answer generation to achieve 92% answer accuracy.
features: Indexes and reasons over more than 100,000 product records
Models related products through a product association graph
Retrieves graph neighborhoods relevant to each customer question
Combines structured relationships with RAG-based context retrieval
Generates context-aware answers grounded in product data
Handles product comparison, compatibility, and discovery questions

name: CodeAtlas
description: A local-first code knowledge graph for AI-assisted software development that scans repositories, extracts structural relationships, and serves bounded context for debugging, review, impact analysis, and coding-agent workflows.
features: Builds persistent code knowledge graphs from local repositories
Extracts symbols and structural relationships across Python, Java, JavaScript, TypeScript, JSX, and TSX
Supports symbol lookup, impact analysis, bounded context retrieval, and graph-neighborhood traversal
Performs full builds and changed-file incremental graph updates
Stores graph state locally in SQLite with full-text search
Exposes a CLI and stable MCP tools for Codex and other agent clients
Includes FastAPI services plus React dashboard views for repository insights, 2D and 3D graph exploration, and code queries
